title: Figure out while loop — Oct 2022 CS-coursework one-liner
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only note from Oct 13, 2022: "Figure out while loop." Empty body. Pure title-only sub-pattern.
  • Earliest in-the-trenches CS coursework friction point captured in vault — Yahir at 19, sophomore-year OSU CS, hitting the most basic control-flow construct as a roadblock worth noting.
  • Two days before the Ahmad Jamal mega dump — locates Yahir in mid-October 2022 build-mode, when OSIG todos and CS-161/162 assignments were stacked on the same surface.
  • Connects to the June Jackson graph theory questions as the content-side of Yahir's CS-major experience — most coursework notes are admin-only ("do MTH quiz"), but this and the Jackson note are rare topic-level captures.
  • The brevity is the artifact: Yahir didn't write what he didn't understand about the while-loop, just that he needed to come back to it. Catch-the-obstacle-in-flight without dignifying it with explanation.

Why it matters

A small but load-bearing exhibit of Yahir as a late CS bloomer — three years before rankett, the same person who'd architect a multi-tenant white-label platform was stuck on while. Important context for the founder arc: Yahir's technical instincts in 2026 are not native; they're earned. The 2022 is full of these tiny "I don't get X yet" admissions — see also the Jackson discrete math questions from four months earlier.

Open questions

  • Which class? Likely cs 161 (intro to CS-I) or cs 162 (intro to CS-II) at OSU.
  • Did Yahir resolve it that night, or did the loop confusion persist?
  • Is there a CS coursework cluster worth spinning into a synthesis page tracking the Yahir-as-engineer arc from 2021–2026?
